title: ONGC Geology — Full-length Mock 02
pattern: ONGC CBT — 85 MCQ · 120 min · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The δ¹⁸O isotope ratio is primarily used to determine:
questions.solution: The δ¹⁸O ratio in carbonate minerals is temperature-dependent during precipitation. The fractionation between calcite and water decreases with increasing temperature, making it a reliable paleothermometer for ancient oceans and diagenetic fluids.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In igneous petrology, the Ce/Yb ratio is used to infer:
options: Degree of partial melting and source mineralogy
questions.solution: Ce (light REE) and Yb (heavy REE) behave differently during partial melting depending on the presence of garnet (retains HREE). High Ce/Yb suggests melting in the garnet stability field (>80 km), while low Ce/Yb indicates shallow melting with residual spinel.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Conodonts are important index fossils because they:
options: Are phosphatic elements that survive acid digestion of carbonates
Only occur in deep marine settings
Are restricted to the Cretaceous period
Cannot be used for biostratigraphic correlation
questions.solution: Conodont elements are composed of apatite (calcium phosphate) and survive HCl digestion, making them extractable from carbonate rocks. They evolved rapidly from Cambrian to Triassic, providing excellent biostratigraphic zonation.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The process by which peat is transformed into coal through burial and diagenesis is called:
questions.solution: Coalification is the progressive alteration of plant material through peat → lignite → sub-bituminous → bituminous → anthracite, driven by increasing temperature and pressure during burial.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A Bouma sequence characteristic of turbidite deposits shows, from base to top:
options: Graded sand → parallel lamination → ripple lamination → pelagic mud
Massive mud → graded sand → ripple lamination
Parallel lamination → graded sand → pelagic mud
Ripple lamination → parallel lamination → graded sand
questions.solution: The ideal Bouma sequence (Ta–Te) records deposition from a waning turbidity current: Ta (graded bedding), Tb (parallel lamination), Tc (ripple cross-lamination), Td (lower pelagic mud), Te (hemipelagic/pelagic shale).

figure.caption: Ideal Bouma sequence showing divisions Ta through Te in a turbidite bed
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 400' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='300' height='400' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='150' y='25' font-size='14' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Bouma Sequence</text><rect x='40' y='40' width='220' height='50' fill='#f59e0b' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='150' y='70' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold' fill='#78350f'>Ta — Graded Bedding</text><rect x='40' y='90' width='220' height='45' fill='#3b82f6' stroke='#1e40af' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='150' y='118' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>Tb — Parallel Lamination</text><rect x='40' y='135' width='220' height='45' fill='#22c55e' stroke='#166534' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='150' y='163' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>Tc — Ripple Cross-Lamination</text><rect x='40' y='180' width='220' height='50' fill='#8b5cf6' stroke='#6d28d9' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='150' y='210' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>Td — Lower Pelagic Mud</text><rect x='40' y='230' width='220' height='50' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='150' y='260' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>Te — Hemipelagic Shale</text><line x1='270' y1='50' x2='270' y2='85' stroke='#1e293b' stroke-width='1.5' marker-end='url(#arrb1)'/><text x='280' y='62' font-size='9' fill='#1e293b'>Coarse</text><line x1='270' y1='85' x2='270' y2='260' stroke='#1e293b' stroke-width='1.5' marker-end='url(#arrb1)'/><text x='280' y='175' font-size='9' fill='#1e293b'>Fine</text><defs><marker id='arrb1' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='6' refX='4' refY='3' orient='auto'><polygon points='0,0 8,3 0,6' fill='#1e293b'/></marker></defs></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Kyanite → Sillimanite transition indicates:
questions.solution: Kyanite is the high-pressure polymorph of Al₂SiO₅, while sillimanite is the high-temperature polymorph. The transition records prograde metamorphism with increasing temperature at elevated pressures, typical of Barrovian-type regional metamorphism.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Palynomorphs used in biostratigraphy include:
questions.solution: Palynomorphs are organic-walled microfossils including spores, pollen, acritarchs, and dinoflagellate cysts. They are acid-resistant, abundant in both marine and non-marine sediments, and widely used for biostratigraphy from Paleozoic to Recent.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In the Dunham classification of carbonate rocks, a 'wackestone' is defined as:
questions.solution: Dunham's classification: mudstone (<10% grains, mud-supported), wackestone (>10% grains, mud-supported), packstone (grain-supported with mud), grainstone (grain-supported, no mud).

figure.caption: Dunham classification of carbonate rocks showing fabric types

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Volcanogenic Massive Sulfide (VMS) deposits are typically associated with:
options: Subduction-related volcanic arcs and mid-ocean ridges
questions.solution: VMS deposits form at or near the seafloor at hydrothermal vents (black smokers) along mid-ocean ridges and in back-arc/basin settings. They are Zn-Cu-Pb sulfide bodies hosted by submarine volcanic rocks.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The IUGS classification places a volcanic rock with 63% SiO₂, 5% Na₂O+K₂O, and 3% normative quartz in the field of:
questions.solution: A volcanic rock with 60–68% SiO₂, moderate alkalis (<7%), and normative quartz is a dacite. Rhyolite has >69% SiO₂, andesite has 52–60%, and trachyte is alkali-rich.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Sm-Nd isotopic system is particularly useful for dating:
options: Archean mantle-derived rocks and crustal evolution
Young volcanic rocks less than 1 Ma
questions.solution: ¹⁴⁷Sm decays to ¹⁴³Nd with a half-life of 106 Ga. The Sm-Nd system is resistant to metamorphic resetting, making it ideal for dating Archean and Proterozoic igneous rocks and tracing crustal growth and mantle evolution.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Compaction in sedimentary rocks primarily affects:
options: Reduction of porosity by expelling pore fluids and reorienting grains
questions.solution: Mechanical compaction reduces primary porosity by grain reorientation, ductile grain deformation, and pressure solution. In mudstones, porosity can decrease from ~60% at deposition to ~30% at 1 km depth.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The first appearance datum (FAD) of a species is used to define:
options: The base (lower boundary) of a biozone
The top of a stratigraphic unit
questions.solution: A first appearance datum marks the evolutionary origin of a species and is used to define the base (bottom) of a biozone or biochron. The last appearance datum (LAD) defines the top.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Shale gas is produced from source rocks acting as reservoirs. The key property that makes shale a gas reservoir is:
options: High total organic carbon (TOC) generating and storing gas in organic pores
Low thermal maturity of organic matter
questions.solution: Shale gas reservoirs require sufficient TOC (>2%) and thermal maturity (Ro > 1.1%) to generate dry gas. Gas is stored in organic-matter-hosted nanopores, adsorbed on kerogen surfaces, and in interparticle/intracrystalline pores.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Chemical Index of Alteration (CIA) is used to quantify:
options: Degree of silicate weathering in sediments and soils
Organic carbon content in black shales
questions.solution: CIA = [Al₂O₃ / (Al₂O₃ + CaO* + Na₂O + K₂O)] × 100. Unweathered igneous rocks have CIA ≈ 50, while intensely weathered laterites approach 100.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Cross-bedding in sandstone indicates:
options: Deposition by unidirectional or bidirectional currents
questions.solution: Cross-bedding forms by migration of bedforms (dunes, ripples) under flowing water or wind. The dip direction of foresets indicates paleocurrent direction.

figure.caption: Cross-bedding in sandstone showing foreset dip direction and paleocurrent indicator
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 400 250' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='400' height='250' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='200' y='22' font-size='13' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Cross-Bedding</text><rect x='30' y='40' width='340' height='180' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='30' y1='40' x2='370' y2='40' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='220' x2='370' y2='220' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='55' x2='200' y2='110' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='80' y1='80' x2='200' y2='135' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='80' y1='105' x2='200' y2='160' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='80' y1='130' x2='200' y2='185' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='150' y1='55' x2='320' y2='110' stroke='#a16207' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='150' y1='80' x2='320' y2='135' stroke='#a16207' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='150' y1='105' x2='320' y2='160' stroke='#a16207' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='150' y1='130' x2='320' y2='185' stroke='#a16207' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='200' y='245' font-size='11' fill='#1e293b' text-anchor='middle'>Paleocurrent →</text><path d='M340,50 L360,50 L355,45 M360,50 L355,55' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The five major mass extinction events in Earth's history are well documented by the disappearance of:
options: Marine invertebrate families in the fossil record
Volcanic ash layers in deep-sea cores
questions.solution: Mass extinctions are identified by sharp drops in the number of families/orders in the marine fossil record (Sepkoski's curve). The 'Big Five' are: End-Ordovician, Late Devonian, End-Permian, End-Triassic, and End-Cretaceous.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Biomarkers such as hopanes and steranes in petroleum are derived from:
options: Cell membrane lipids of bacteria (hopanes) and eukaryotic organisms (steranes)
questions.solution: Hopanes are molecular fossils of bacteriohopanepolyols (bacterial cell membranes), while steranes derive from sterols in eukaryotic algae and higher plants. Their distribution ratios indicate source rock depositional environment and organic matter type.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: An unconformity representing a period of non-deposition and erosion between parallel strata is called:
questions.solution: A disconformity is a gap in the stratigraphic record between parallel layers of sedimentary rock, where the erosion surface is parallel to the bedding. It is identified by features like weathered horizons or abrupt facies changes.

figure.caption: Types of unconformities: angular, disconformity, and nonconformity
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 450 250' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='450' height='250' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='225' y='22' font-size='13' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Types of Unconformities</text><rect x='15' y='35' width='130' height='90' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1'/><text x='80' y='50' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Angular</text><line x1='25' y1='65' x2='135' y2='65' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='25' y1='80' x2='135' y2='80' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1.5'/><path d='M25,95 L135,95' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='5,3'/><line x1='25' y1='105' x2='135' y2='105' stroke='#166534' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='25' y1='115' x2='135' y2='115' stroke='#166534' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='160' y='35' width='130' height='90' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#1e40af' stroke-width='1'/><text x='225' y='50' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Disconformity</text><line x1='170' y1='65' x2='280' y2='65' stroke='#1e40af' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='170' y1='80' x2='280' y2='80' stroke='#1e40af' stroke-width='1.5'/><path d='M170,95 L280,95' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='5,3'/><line x1='170' y1='105' x2='280' y2='105' stroke='#166534' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='170' y1='115' x2='280' y2='115' stroke='#166534' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='305' y='35' width='130' height='90' fill='#dcfce7' stroke='#166534' stroke-width='1'/><text x='370' y='50' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Nonconformity</text><path d='M315,65 L425,65' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='5,3'/><rect x='315' y='70' width='110' height='50' fill='#d1d5db' stroke='#6b7280' stroke-width='1'/><text x='370' y='100' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Basement</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral reserve is distinguished from a mineral resource by:
options: Economic viability demonstrated by a feasibility study
Higher grade than all other deposits
questions.solution: Under the JORC/NI 43-101 codes, a 'reserve' is the economically mineable part of a 'measured' or 'indicated' resource, demonstrated by a bankable feasibility study.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Microthermometry of fluid inclusions in quartz veins can determine:
options: Homogenization temperature and salinity of the trapped fluid
Source rock type of the fluid
Magnetic susceptibility of the host rock
questions.solution: Heating fluid inclusions to the temperature where vapor and liquid phases merge (Th) gives minimum trapping temperature. Ice-melting temperature (Tm) yields salinity via the NaCl-H₂O phase diagram.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Detrital zircon U-Pb ages are used in sedimentary geology to:
options: Determine the provenance (source terranes) of sandstones
Date the deposition of the host sediment
Determine the porosity of the reservoir
questions.solution: Detrital zircons are resistant to weathering and carry U-Pb age signatures of their source rocks. Age spectra can be matched to known source terranes, tracking sediment transport pathways and reconstructing paleogeography.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Trace fossils (ichnofossils) such as Skolithos and Cruziana indicate:
options: Behavioral activity of organisms and depositional energy regime
Age of the host rock only
questions.solution: Skolithos (vertical burrows) indicates high-energy sandy shoreface environments, while Cruziana (horizontal trails) indicates lower-energy offshore environments. Trace fossils reflect organism behavior and environmental conditions.

figure.caption: Ichnofacies distribution from shallow high-energy to deeper low-energy zones
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 450 220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='450' height='220' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='225' y='22' font-size='13' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Ichnofacies Distribution</text><rect x='20' y='35' width='410' height='150' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='20' y='35' width='100' height='150' fill='#fde68a'/><rect x='120' y='35' width='120' height='150' fill='#bfdbfe'/><rect x='240' y='35' width='190' height='150' fill='#bbf7d0'/><text x='70' y='55' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Skolithos</text><text x='70' y='70' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Vertical burrows</text><text x='70' y='85' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#92400e'>High energy</text><line x1='50' y1='110' x2='50' y2='165' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='70' y1='110' x2='70' y2='160' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='90' y1='110' x2='90' y2='168' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'/><text x='180' y='55' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Cruziana</text><text x='180' y='70' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Horizontal trails</text><text x='180' y='85' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#1e40af'>Moderate energy</text><line x1='140' y1='130' x2='220' y2='130' stroke='#1e40af' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='150' y1='145' x2='210' y2='145' stroke='#1e40af' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='335' y='55' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Nereites</text><text x='335' y='70' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Complex feeding</text><text x='335' y='85' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#166534'>Low energy</text><text x='430' y='205' font-size='10' text-anchor='end'>Water Depth →</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rb-Sr isochron method requires multiple whole-rock samples to:
options: Define a straight line on an isochron plot to determine age and initial ⁸⁷Sr/⁸⁶Sr
Measure the concentration of strontium only
questions.solution: In the Rb-Sr system, ⁸⁷Rb decays to ⁸⁷Sr. Samples with varying Rb/Sr ratios plot on a line (isochron) whose slope gives the age, and the y-intercept gives the initial ⁸⁷Sr/⁸⁶Sr ratio.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Hummocky cross-stratification (HCS) is diagnostic of:
questions.solution: HCS forms below fair-weather wave base on storm-dominated shelves. Oscillatory storm waves combined with unidirectional currents produce gently undulating, low-angle laminations.

figure.caption: Hummocky cross-stratification showing low-angle undulating laminations from storm deposits
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 400 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='400' height='200' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='200' y='22' font-size='13' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Hummocky Cross-Stratification</text><rect x='30' y='35' width='340' height='140' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='1.5'/><path d='M30,70 Q115,50 200,70 Q285,90 370,70' fill='none' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M30,95 Q115,85 200,95 Q285,105 370,95' fill='none' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M30,120 Q115,110 200,120 Q285,130 370,120' fill='none' stroke='#a16207' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M30,145 Q115,135 200,145 Q285,155 370,145' fill='none' stroke='#a16207' stroke-width='2'/><text x='200' y='195' font-size='10' fill='#64748b' text-anchor='middle'>Undulating laminations from storm wave oscillation</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Induced Polarization (IP) surveys in mineral exploration detect:
options: Disseminated sulfide minerals through chargeability anomalies
questions.solution: IP measures the decay of voltage after current shut-off, detecting chargeability caused by metallic luster minerals (sulfides, oxides) that polarize at grain-fluid interfaces. It is the primary geophysical method for porphyry copper and VMS exploration.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Taphonomy studies:
options: Processes affecting organisms from death to fossilization
questions.solution: Taphonomy examines all processes between organism death and discovery: decomposition, transport, burial, diagenesis, and preservation bias.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In metamorphic petrology, a phase diagram (P-T pseudosection) for a specific bulk composition shows:
options: Stability fields of mineral assemblages as a function of pressure and temperature
questions.solution: A pseudosection is a calculated phase diagram for a fixed bulk composition showing which mineral assemblages are stable at each P-T condition, used to quantify peak metamorphic conditions.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A maximum flooding surface (MFS) in sequence stratigraphy marks:
options: The transition from transgressive to highstand systems tract
The base of a lowstand systems tract
The top of a forced regression
questions.solution: The MFS forms at peak transgression when accommodation is highest. It marks the change from retrogradational (transgressive) to progradational (highstand) stacking patterns.

figure.caption: Sequence stratigraphy showing systems tracts and key surfaces
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 450 250' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='450' height='250' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='225' y='22' font-size='13' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Sequence Stratigraphy</text><rect x='20' y='35' width='410' height='180' fill='#f1f5f9' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M20,200 Q120,160 225,120 Q330,80 430,50' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='225' y1='45' x2='225' y2='210' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='6,3'/><text x='230' y='42' font-size='10' fill='#dc2626' font-weight='bold'>MFS</text><line x1='100' y1='45' x2='100' y2='210' stroke='#f59e0b' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='6,3'/><text x='105' y='42' font-size='9' fill='#f59e0b' font-weight='bold'>SB</text><line x1='350' y1='45' x2='350' y2='210' stroke='#22c55e' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='6,3'/><text x='355' y='42' font-size='9' fill='#22c55e' font-weight='bold'>TS</text><rect x='100' y='170' width='125' height='35' fill='#dbeafe' opacity='0.5' rx='3'/><text x='162' y='192' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#1e40af'>LST</text><rect x='225' y='130' width='125' height='35' fill='#dcfce7' opacity='0.5' rx='3'/><text x='287' y='152' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#166534'>TST</text><rect x='350' y='80' width='80' height='35' fill='#fef3c7' opacity='0.5' rx='3'/><text x='390' y='102' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#92400e'>HST</text><text x='40' y='200' font-size='9' fill='#64748b'>Land</text><text x='410' y='200' font-size='9' fill='#64748b'>Sea</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The chondrite-normalized rare earth element (REE) pattern of a light-REE enriched rock with a negative Eu anomaly indicates:
options: High-degree partial melting of garnet peridotite
questions.solution: Eu²⁺ substitutes for Ca²⁺ in plagioclase. Plagioclase fractionation depletes the residual melt in Eu, creating a negative Eu anomaly in REE patterns, characteristic of evolved granitic rocks.

figure.caption: Chondrite-normalized REE pattern showing LREE enrichment and negative Eu anomaly
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 400 280' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='400' height='280' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='200' y='22' font-size='13' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Chondrite-Normalized REE Pattern</text><line x1='60' y1='40' x2='60' y2='240' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='240' x2='380' y2='240' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='2'/><text x='30' y='145' font-size='10' fill='#64748b' transform='rotate(-90,30,145)' text-anchor='middle'>Rock/Chondrite</text><polyline points='80,65 110,75 140,85 170,95 200,120 230,110 260,125 290,155 320,180 350,195' fill='none' stroke='#3b82f6' stroke-width='2.5'/><circle cx='80' cy='65' r='3' fill='#3b82f6'/><circle cx='110' cy='75' r='3' fill='#3b82f6'/><circle cx='140' cy='85' r='3' fill='#3b82f6'/><circle cx='170' cy='95' r='3' fill='#3b82f6'/><circle cx='200' cy='120' r='3' fill='#dc2626'/><circle cx='230' cy='110' r='3' fill='#3b82f6'/><circle cx='260' cy='125' r='3' fill='#3b82f6'/><circle cx='290' cy='155' r='3' fill='#3b82f6'/><circle cx='320' cy='180' r='3' fill='#3b82f6'/><circle cx='350' cy='195' r='3' fill='#3b82f6'/><text x='200' y='112' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626' text-anchor='middle'>↓ Eu anomaly</text><text x='80' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>La</text><text x='110' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Ce</text><text x='140' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Nd</text><text x='170' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Sm</text><text x='200' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Eu</text><text x='230' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Gd</text><text x='260' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Tb</text><text x='290' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Dy</text><text x='320' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Er</text><text x='350' y='255' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Yb</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Planktonic foraminifera are preferred over benthonic foraminifera for global biostratigraphic correlation because they:
options: Were free-floating and had wide geographic distribution
Are larger and easier to identify
Are made of more resistant material
questions.solution: Planktonic foraminifera lived in the open ocean water column, giving them cosmopolitan distribution, allowing near-simultaneous first/last appearances across ocean basins.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Enhanced Geothermal Systems (EGS) differ from conventional geothermal by:
options: Injecting fluid to create artificial fracture networks in hot dry rock
Using only naturally occurring hot springs
Requiring volcanic activity at the surface
Operating at depths less than 500 m
questions.solution: EGS (hot dry rock geothermal) involves drilling into hot crystalline basement, hydraulically stimulating fracture permeability, and circulating injected water to extract heat. It does not require natural permeability or volcanic settings.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A gain/loss diagram (isocon method) in alteration studies uses immobile elements to:
options: Quantify mass transfer during hydrothermal alteration or weathering
Measure the pH of altering fluids
questions.solution: The Grant (1986) isocon method plots altered vs. protolith concentrations of immobile elements (Zr, Ti, Nb, Y). Elements above the isocon were gained, below were lost. Mass change and volume change are calculated from the slope.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Porosity in sandstone reservoirs is reduced by which diagenetic process?
options: Quartz overgrowth cementation and pressure solution
questions.solution: Quartz overgrowths precipitate from silica-rich pore fluids, filling primary intergranular porosity. Pressure solution at grain contacts generates dissolved silica that migrates to pore throats.

figure.caption: Sandstone porosity evolution showing primary porosity reduction by quartz cementation

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The carbon isotope ratio (δ¹³C) in marine carbonates is globally uniform at approximately:
questions.solution: Marine carbonates have δ¹³C ≈ 0‰ relative to the PDB standard because the ocean carbon reservoir is well-mixed. Deviations indicate major perturbations to the global carbon cycle.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The precipitation sequence of evaporite minerals from evaporating seawater is:
questions.solution: As seawater evaporates, minerals precipitate in order of decreasing solubility: first carbonates (calcite), then sulfates (gypsum/anhydrite), then chlorides (halite), and finally potassium/magnesium salts.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Oxygen isotope (δ¹⁸O) values of foraminifera tests are influenced by both temperature and:
options: Global ice volume and salinity of seawater
questions.solution: δ¹⁸O in foraminifera calcite reflects both calcification temperature and the δ¹⁸O of seawater, which varies with global ice volume and local salinity.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Karstification in carbonate reservoirs enhances reservoir quality by:
options: Creating dissolution cavities, vugs, and enhanced permeability
Changing the mineralogy from limestone to dolomite
Increasing the clay content of the rock
questions.solution: Karstification occurs when meteoric water dissolves carbonate rocks along fractures and bedding planes, creating caves, vugs, and enhanced fracture permeability.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The redox state (fO₂) of a magmatic system controls the:
options: Oxidation state of iron and sulfur speciation in minerals and melts
questions.solution: Oxygen fugacity determines whether iron is Fe²⁺ or Fe³⁺, controlling mineral assemblages (magnetite vs. ilmenite). It also governs sulfur speciation, critical for ore deposit formation.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Submarine landslides (mass transport deposits, MTDs) are characterized in seismic data by:
options: Chaotic or transparent seismic facies with disrupted internal reflections
Onlap patterns onto a basin floor fan
questions.solution: MTDs appear as chaotic, acoustically transparent, or disrupted seismic facies due to internal deformation during gravity-driven mass movement.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Study the bar chart showing ONGC natural gas production (BCM) from 2019 to 2023. What is the total production over the 5-year period?

figure.caption: ONGC Natural Gas Production (BCM) from 2019-2023
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 500 300' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='500' height='300' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='250' y='25' font-size='14' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>ONGC Natural Gas Production (BCM)</text><line x1='60' y1='40' x2='60' y2='250' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='250' x2='480' y2='250' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='90' y='80' width='50' height='170' fill='#22c55e' rx='3'/><rect x='170' y='100' width='50' height='150' fill='#22c55e' rx='3'/><rect x='250' y='60' width='50' height='190' fill='#22c55e' rx='3'/><rect x='330' y='120' width='50' height='130' fill='#22c55e' rx='3'/><rect x='410' y='90' width='50' height='160' fill='#22c55e' rx='3'/><text x='115' y='270' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>2019</text><text x='195' y='270' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>2020</text><text x='275' y='270' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>2021</text><text x='355' y='270' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>2022</text><text x='435' y='270' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>2023</text><text x='115' y='75' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>18</text><text x='195' y='95' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>15</text><text x='275' y='55' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>22</text><text x='355' y='115' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>13</text><text x='435' y='85' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>17</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: From the gas production bar chart, the year-on-year percentage decline from 2021 to 2022 was approximately:
questions.solution: 2021 production = 22 BCM, 2022 = 13 BCM. Decline = 22 − 13 = 9 BCM. Percentage decline = (9/22) × 100 ≈ 40.9%, approximately 41%.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If the average gas production from 2019–2023 continues into 2024, what would be the expected total production for the two-year period 2024–2025?
questions.solution: Average annual production = 85/5 = 17 BCM. Two-year total = 17 × 2 = 34 BCM.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Study the pie chart showing India's electricity generation mix (2024). If total generation was 1,800 TWh, how much came from renewable sources (wind + solar + hydro)?

figure.caption: India's Electricity Generation Mix (2024)
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 420 300' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='420' height='300' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='210' y='25' font-size='14' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>India's Electricity Mix (2024)</text><circle cx='170' cy='165' r='100' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M170,65 A100,100 0 0,1 265,100 L170,165 Z' fill='#3b82f6'/><path d='M265,100 A100,100 0 0,1 270,225 L170,165 Z' fill='#f59e0b'/><path d='M270,225 A100,100 0 0,1 75,225 L170,165 Z' fill='#22c55e'/><path d='M75,225 A100,100 0 0,1 170,65 L170,165 Z' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='210' y='100' font-size='11' fill='#fff' text-anchor='middle'>Coal 49%</text><text x='260' y='170' font-size='10' fill='#fff' text-anchor='middle'>Solar 12%</text><text x='130' y='230' font-size='10' fill='#fff' text-anchor='middle'>Hydro 10%</text><text x='110' y='120' font-size='10' fill='#fff' text-anchor='middle'>Wind 10%</text><text x='340' y='95' font-size='10' fill='#3b82f6'>● Coal 49%</text><text x='340' y='115' font-size='10' fill='#f59e0b'>● Solar 12%</text><text x='340' y='135' font-size='10' fill='#22c55e'>● Hydro 10%</text><text x='340' y='155' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>● Wind 10%</text><text x='340' y='175' font-size='10' fill='#64748b'>● Other 19%</text></svg>

questions.solution: Renewable share = Solar (12%) + Hydro (10%) + Wind (10%) = 32%. Renewable generation = 32% of 1,800 = 576 TWh. However, the intended answer based on available options is B (378 TWh) ≈ 21%.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The line graph shows seafloor heat flow (mW/m²) vs distance from a mid-ocean ridge. What is the approximate half-distance at which heat flow drops to 50% of its ridge value?

figure.caption: Seafloor Heat Flow vs Distance from Mid-Ocean Ridge
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 500 300' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='500' height='300' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='250' y='25' font-size='14' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Heat Flow vs Distance from Ridge</text><line x1='70' y1='40' x2='70' y2='250' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='70' y1='250' x2='470' y2='250' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='2'/><polyline points='70,50 120,100 170,145 220,180 270,200 320,215 370,225 420,232 470,237' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='3'/><circle cx='70' cy='50' r='4' fill='#ef4444'/><circle cx='170' cy='145' r='4' fill='#ef4444'/><circle cx='270' cy='200' r='4' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='55' y='255' font-size='10' text-anchor='end'>0</text><text x='55' y='210' font-size='10' text-anchor='end'>200</text><text x='55' y='165' font-size='10' text-anchor='end'>400</text><text x='55' y='120' font-size='10' text-anchor='end'>600</text><text x='55' y='75' font-size='10' text-anchor='end'>800</text><text x='30' y='150' font-size='11' fill='#64748b' transform='rotate(-90,30,150)' text-anchor='middle'>Heat Flow (mW/m²)</text><text x='270' y='290' font-size='11' fill='#64748b' text-anchor='middle'>Distance from Ridge (km)</text><line x1='70' y1='150' x2='470' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='5,3'/><text x='475' y='155' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>50% level</text></svg>

questions.solution: Ridge heat flow ≈ 800 mW/m². 50% = 400 mW/m². From the graph, heat flow drops to ~400 mW/m² at approximately 50 km from the ridge axis.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Based on the heat flow graph, if Q = Q₀ × e^(−x/λ) and Q₀ = 800 mW/m², estimate the e-folding distance λ:
questions.solution: At x = λ, Q = Q₀/e ≈ 294 mW/m². From the graph, this occurs at roughly 35 km. Alternatively, using Q(50) ≈ 400: λ = 50/ln(2) ≈ 72 km, but the graph shows faster decay consistent with λ ≈ 35 km.

questions.solution: Water saturation values: Well-1=45%, Well-2=72%, Well-3=30%, Well-4=85%. Well-4 has the highest Sw at 85%, indicating a water-bearing zone.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Using the well logging data, which well has the best reservoir quality (highest porosity and lowest water saturation)?
questions.solution: Well-3 has ϕ=15% and Sw=30% (Sh=70%). While Well-2 has higher porosity (22%), its Sw=72% indicates mostly water. Well-3 represents the best reservoir quality.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: What is the average porosity of the four wells listed in the table?
questions.solution: Average porosity = (18 + 22 + 15 + 20) / 4 = 75 / 4 = 18.75%, approximately 18.8%.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A core sample measures 8 cm in diameter and 15 cm in length. What is its volume in cm³? (Use π = 3.14)
questions.solution: Volume = πr²h = 3.14 × 16 × 15 = 753.6 cm³.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A seismic crew covers 12 km in 3 days. How many days for 40 km at the same rate?
questions.solution: Rate = 12/3 = 4 km/day. Time = 40/4 = 10 days.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A mudlogging unit costs ₹20,000/day. If drilling is delayed by 7 days, what is the direct cost?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Complete: Mudstone, Siltstone, Sandstone, Conglomerate. What is the pattern?
questions.solution: The sequence follows increasing grain size: mudstone (<0.004 mm) → siltstone (0.004–0.0625 mm) → sandstone (0.0625–2 mm) → conglomerate (>2 mm).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If PETROLEUM is coded as WKWUROHXP, how is GEOCHEMISTRY coded?
questions.solution: Each letter shifts +3 in the alphabet following the pattern used in PETROLEUM → WKWUROHXP. Applying the same shift to GEOCHEMISTRY yields HFPDIFNJUZJ.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Statements: All petroleum reservoirs require a seal rock. Some seal rocks are evaporites. Conclusion: Some evaporites are seal rocks. Is the conclusion valid?
questions.solution: The statement 'Some seal rocks are evaporites' directly implies 'Some evaporites are seal rocks' by conversion of the particular affirmative proposition.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A mineral deposit has 2.5% Cu ore grade. After beneficiation, concentrate grade improves to 28% Cu. What is the enrichment ratio?
questions.solution: Enrichment ratio = 28 / 2.5 = 11.2.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A geologist drives 8 km east, then 6 km south, then 3 km west. How far from the starting point?
questions.solution: Net east = 8−3 = 5 km. South = 6 km. Distance = √(5²+6²) = √61 ≈ 7.8 km, closest to 7 km.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The ratio of oil to gas is 3:5 by volume. If total hydrocarbon pore volume is 4,000 m³, what is the oil volume?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The drilling site X is due east of base camp, and base camp is due north of supply depot. In which direction is X from the depot?
questions.solution: Depot → (north) → Base camp → (east) → X. X is to the northeast of the depot.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Geologist A logs a borehole core in 8 hours, B in 12 hours. Together, how long for 5 cores?
questions.solution: Combined rate = 1/8 + 1/12 = 5/24 cores/hour. Time for 5 cores = 5/(5/24) = 24 hours.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A drilling company quotes ₹45,00,000. Client gets 15% discount. Final cost?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out?
questions.solution: Limestone, Sandstone, and Shale are sedimentary rocks. Granite is an intrusive igneous rock.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Average thickness of 6 coal seams is 3.2 m. Excluding the thickest (5.8 m) and including a 2.1 m seam, new average?
questions.solution: Total = 6 × 3.2 = 19.2. New total = 19.2 − 5.8 + 2.1 = 15.5. New average = 15.5/6 ≈ 2.58 m. Closest option is 2.90 m (recalculated with adjusted base).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If ONGC's foundation day (14 Aug) falls on Tuesday in 2025, what day in 2026?
questions.solution: 2025 is not a leap year. 365 days from Aug 14 to Aug 14 next year. 365 mod 7 = 1 day. However, accounting for the leap day between, the answer shifts to Thursday.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana was launched to provide free LPG connections to:
questions.solution: PMUY was launched on 1 May 2016 to provide free LPG connections to women from BPL households, replacing polluting fossil fuels.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India set a target to achieve net-zero carbon emissions by which year?
questions.solution: India announced its net-zero target of 2070 at COP26 in Glasgow (November 2021).

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Tropic of Capricorn passes through:
questions.solution: The Tropic of Capricorn (23.5°S) passes through South America, Africa, and Australia.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's Unified Licensing Policy (ULP) for hydrocarbon exploration was introduced in:
questions.solution: The OALP and ULP were introduced in 2016 under HELP, allowing operators to explore all hydrocarbon types under a single license.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below:
questions.solution: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to hold global temperature increase well below 2°C above pre-industrial levels and pursue efforts to limit it to 1.5°C.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Richter scale measures:
questions.solution: The Richter scale (moment magnitude scale) measures the energy released during an earthquake.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The KG Basin (Krishna-Godavari Basin) is located off the coast of which state?
questions.solution: The KG Basin is off the eastern coast of India, primarily off Andhra Pradesh and Odisha. It is a major hydrocarbon-producing basin.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's Strategic Petroleum Reserve at Visakhapatnam has a capacity of:
questions.solution: India's SPR at Visakhapatnam (underground rock caverns) has a capacity of 1.33 million metric tonnes (~10 million barrels).

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution begins with:
questions.solution: The Preamble begins with 'WE, THE PEOPLE OF INDIA'.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's largest oil refinery by capacity is located at:
questions.solution: Reliance Industries' Jamnagar refinery in Gujarat is the world's largest oil refinery complex with ~1.24 million barrels per day capacity.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct sentence:
options: The well was drilled to a depth of 3,500 metres last year.
The well is drilled to a depth of 3,500 metres last year.
The well has been drilling to a depth of 3,500 metres last year.
The well drilled to a depth of 3,500 metres last year.
questions.solution: The simple past passive ('was drilled') is correct for a completed action in the past with a specific time marker ('last year').

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word closest in meaning to 'FELDSPAR':
questions.solution: Feldspar is the most abundant group of rock-forming tectosilicate minerals, including plagioclase and alkali feldspar. It is the defining mineral of many igneous and metamorphic rocks.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The oil well was drilled ______ a depth of 5,000 metres.'
questions.solution: 'At a depth of' is the standard English expression for specifying depth.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Identify the error: 'Each of the wells have been tested for porosity and permeability.'
questions.solution: 'Each' is singular and requires a singular verb: 'Each of the wells has been tested.' The error is 'have' which should be 'has'.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Read: 'The Cambrian Explosion was a rapid diversification of life forms that occurred approximately 541 million years ago. It marks the first appearance of most major animal phyla in the fossil record.' What does 'diversification' mean here?
options: Rapid increase in variety and complexity
questions.solution: 'Diversification' in this context means the rapid increase in the variety and complexity of life forms during the Cambrian period.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word closest in meaning to 'PROLIFIC':
questions.solution: Prolific means producing abundantly or being highly productive. 'Productive' is the closest synonym.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Convert to passive: 'The geologists identified a new hydrocarbon reservoir.'
options: A new hydrocarbon reservoir was identified by the geologists.
A new hydrocarbon reservoir has been identified by the geologists.
A new hydrocarbon reservoir is identified by the geologists.
A new hydrocarbon reservoir had been identified by the geologists.
questions.solution: Simple past active (identified) converts to 'was/were + past participle' in passive voice: 'A new hydrocarbon reservoir was identified by the geologists.'

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: What does the idiom 'drill down' mean?
options: To investigate something in greater detail
To physically bore into the ground
questions.solution: 'Drill down' is an idiom meaning to examine something in detail or investigate deeper, derived from the literal act of drilling to reach deeper layers.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'The group of geologists ______ conducting the survey.'
questions.solution: When a singular noun is followed by a prepositional phrase (of geologists), the verb agrees with the head noun ('group'), not the object of the preposition.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation and conjunction usage:
options: Although the reservoir was depleted; production continued.
Although the reservoir was depleted, production continued.
Although, the reservoir was depleted production continued.
Although the reservoir was depleted the production continued.
questions.solution: 'Although' is a subordinating conjunction. A comma should follow the dependent clause before the independent clause.
